title: Uparkot Fort

topPlaces.name: Navghan Kuvo
topPlaces.description: Navghan Kuvo is a stepwell in Uparkot Fort, Junagadh, Gujarat, India.

topPlaces.name: Jumma Masjid, Uparkot
topPlaces.description: The Jumma Masjid or Jama Masjid is a former Friday mosque, now in partial ruins, in Uparkot Fort in Junagadh, in the state of Gujarat, India. The mosque was built in 15th century by converting a temple or a palace identified as Ranakdevi Mahal by local people and the Archaeological Survey of India.

topPlaces.name: Uparkot Caves
topPlaces.description: Uparkot caves, also Uperkot caves, are ancient man-made caverns. The caves are a part of the Junagadh Buddhist Cave Groups situated in the eastern part of Junagadh of the Indian state of Gujarat.

topPlaces.name: Adi Kadi Vav
topPlaces.description: Adi Kadi Vav or Adi Chadi Vav is a stepwell in Uparkot Fort, Junagadh, Gujarat, India. The date of its construction is uncertain.

topPlaces.name: Junagadh State
topPlaces.description: The State of Junagadh was a princely state in the Kathiawar Peninsula of western India, located in present-day Gujarat. Ruled by the Babi dynasty of Muslim Nawabs since 1654, it was one of the larger princely states in the region firstly within the Maratha Empire and later by British India.

topPlaces.name: Bava Pyara Caves
topPlaces.description: Bava Pyara caves (also known as Baba Pyara caves) are an example of ancient man-made caverns. The caves are a part of the Junagadh Buddhist Cave Groups situated in the eastern part of Junagadh of the Indian state of Gujarat.

topPlaces.name: Khapra Kodiya Caves
topPlaces.description: The Khapra Kodiya Caves are part of the Junagadh Buddhist Cave Group. They are the oldest of the caves in the group.

topPlaces.name: Mahabat Maqbara complex
topPlaces.description: Mahabat Maqbara and Bahauddin Maqbra are mausoleums in Junagadh, Gujarat, India. They were completed in 1892 and 1896 respectively and are dedicated to Mahabat Khan II, the Nawab of Junagadh State, and his minister Bahauddin Hussain Bhar respectively.

items.note: Navghan Kuvo is a stepwell in Uparkot Fort, Junagadh, Gujarat, India.

The Jumma Masjid or Jama Masjid is a former Friday mosque, now in partial ruins, in Uparkot Fort in Junagadh, in the state of Gujarat, India. The mosque was built in 15th century by converting a temple or a palace identified as Ranakdevi Mahal by local people and the Archaeological Survey of India.
Uparkot caves, also Uperkot caves, are ancient man-made caverns. The caves are a part of the Junagadh Buddhist Cave Groups situated in the eastern part of Junagadh of the Indian state of Gujarat.
Adi Kadi Vav or Adi Chadi Vav is a stepwell in Uparkot Fort, Junagadh, Gujarat, India. The date of its construction is uncertain.

The State of Junagadh was a princely state in the Kathiawar Peninsula of western India, located in present-day Gujarat. Ruled by the Babi dynasty of Muslim Nawabs since 1654, it was one of the larger princely states in the region firstly within the Maratha Empire and later by British India.

Bava Pyara caves (also known as Baba Pyara caves) are an example of ancient man-made caverns. The caves are a part of the Junagadh Buddhist Cave Groups situated in the eastern part of Junagadh of the Indian state of Gujarat.
The Khapra Kodiya Caves are part of the Junagadh Buddhist Cave Group. They are the oldest of the caves in the group.

Mahabat Maqbara and Bahauddin Maqbra are mausoleums in Junagadh, Gujarat, India. They were completed in 1892 and 1896 respectively and are dedicated to Mahabat Khan II, the Nawab of Junagadh State, and his minister Bahauddin Hussain Bhar respectively.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Uparkot Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Uparkot Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Uparkot Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Porbandar Airport (85 km) and the nearest railway station is Rajkot Junction (91 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Uparkot Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Uparkot Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Uparkot Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Uparkot Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Uparkot Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Navghan Kuvo, Jumma Masjid, Uparkot, Uparkot Caves, Adi Kadi Vav, Junagadh State.
